guides.title: Introduction
guides.description: Collet is a zero-hydration web UI component library with 49 accessible components compiled to WebAssembly and delivered as Custom Elements.

guides.title: Installation
guides.description: Install @colletdev/core and the wrapper package for your framework.

guides.title: Usage
guides.description: Render first, initialize Collet lazily, and understand startup, selective registration, and runtime loading behavior.

guides.title: SEAM Demo
guides.description: Repo-local proof of concept for ambient session, file, and task-triggered context pings tied to real Collet docs invariants.

guides.title: Messages
guides.description: Understand timeline-oriented message composition with Message Bubble, Message Group, Message Part, Activity Group, and Code Block.

guides.title: Conventions
guides.description: Shared vocabulary and authoring conventions for readable Collet code.

guides.title: Migrating from shadcn/ui
guides.description: Map shadcn/ui and underlying Radix patterns to Collet’s typed component layer, tokens, and lower-context workflow.

guides.title: Theming
guides.description: Theme Collet with CSS custom properties, dark-mode tokens, and ::part() selectors.

components.title: Card
components.description: Surface container with header, body, and footer slots. Renders as <article> in Shadow DOM.
notes: elevated uses --color-surface-raised + shadow.
outlined uses --color-surface + --color-border.
filled uses --color-surface-raised with no border.

components.title: Text
components.description: Semantic typography primitive with role-based sizing, alignment, color, truncation, and line clamping. Renders the correct HTML element via WASM.

props.name: textRole
props.description: Typography role — controls font family, size, weight, and line height

props.name: lineClamp
props.description: Multi-line clamp (e.g. 2 = max 2 lines with ellipsis)

props.name: balance
props.description: CSS text-wrap: balance for even line lengths

props.name: prose
props.description: Enable prose typography (margins, spacing for long-form content)

notes: display role uses --font-display (Instrument Serif); all others use --font-sans (Inter Variable).
code role uses --font-mono (JetBrains Mono).
The host element is display: contents — it does not create a box in the layout.
Use elementAs to override the rendered HTML tag when the default semantic element does not fit.
balance applies CSS text-wrap: balance — best for headings and short paragraphs.

components.title: Sidebar
components.description: Persistent navigation with responsive drawer. Supports groups, icons, active states, and collapse.

components.title: Collapsible
components.description: Show/hide content with an animated toggle trigger.

components.title: Avatar
components.description: User or entity representation with image or initials fallback.

components.title: Scrollbar
components.description: Custom styled scrollable container with visible scrollbar tracks.
notes: The height prop accepts a Tailwind class (e.g. "h-48") applied inside Shadow DOM.
CSS-only fallback: thumb visible at 20% opacity before JS initializes.
JS-enhanced: precise thumb sizing, drag support, and auto-hide on idle.

components.title: Carousel
components.description: Swipeable slide gallery with indicators and navigation arrows.

components.title: Button
components.description: Trigger actions with accessible, styled buttons. Supports text, icon-only, icon+text, toggle, and link modes.
notes: Icon-only buttons require ariaLabel for accessibility.
Icon names use Lucide icon set in kebab-case.
size="sm" icon-only = 32×32px, size="md" = 36×36px.

components.title: Badge
components.description: Compact label for status, counts, or categories. Supports text, dot, and dismissible variants.

props.name: dot
props.description: Show status dot instead of text

components.title: Toggle Group
components.description: Single or multi-select button group. Useful for alignment, view modes, or formatting controls.

components.title: Split Button
components.description: Primary action with dropdown alternatives. The main button performs the default action; the chevron opens a secondary menu.

props.name: entries
props.description: { type, id, label, icon?, intent?, disabled? }

components.title: FAB
components.description: Floating Action Button for primary mobile actions. Supports icon-only and extended (icon + label) modes.
notes: Icon-only FABs require ariaLabel.
Extended FABs show icon + label side by side.

components.title: Speed Dial
components.description: FAB with radiating action menu. Actions fan out in a direction with staggered animation and optional backdrop.

components.title: Text Input
components.description: Single-line and multiline text entry with built-in validation, icons, clearable, and password toggle.

props.name: clearable
props.description: Show clear button when has value

events.name: onInput
events.description: Fires on each keystroke — { value: string }

notes: Filled variant uses --color-secondary for background, NOT --color-surface.
Use kind="multiline" for textarea behavior.
Controlled pattern: value + onInput handler.

components.title: Select
components.description: Dropdown selection from a list of options. Supports single/multi mode and option groups.

components.title: Autocomplete
components.description: Combobox with built-in fuzzy filtering. Supports single/multi select with chips.
notes: Filtering is client-side fuzzy matching on label text.
For server-side search, update items array in onInput handler.

components.title: Checkbox
components.description: Binary or tri-state selection control. Supports checked, unchecked, and indeterminate states.
notes: checked is a STRING, not a boolean. Use "checked", "indeterminate", or undefined.
checked="" (empty string) maps to "checked" — not unchecked!
For unchecked state, use checked={undefined}.

components.title: Switch
components.description: Toggle between on and off states. Unlike Checkbox, checked is a boolean.

props.name: unlabeled
props.description: Hide visible label (label still required for ARIA)

events.name: onInput
events.description: Fires immediately on toggle — { checked: boolean }

notes: Unlike Checkbox, Switch checked is a BOOLEAN.
Supports three shapes: sharp (square track), rounded, and pill (default).
Thumb slides with a CSS transition — WASM handles initial render, JS handles toggle animation.

components.title: Radio Group
components.description: Single selection from a set of options. Uses fieldset/legend for proper semantics.

components.title: Slider
components.description: Numeric value selection along a range.

events.name: onInput
events.description: Fires continuously while dragging — { value: number }

components.title: Chat Input
components.description: Multi-line text area optimized for chat message composition. Auto-grows with content.
notes: Form-associated — participates in native <form> submission via ElementInternals.
Ref exposes focus() method.

components.title: Date Picker
components.description: Calendar-based date input with keyboard navigation and locale support.
notes: Form-associated — participates in native <form> submission.
Ref exposes open(), close(), and focus() methods.

components.title: Search Bar
components.description: Search input with clear button, keyboard shortcut hint, and optional shimmer animation.

props.name: expandable
props.description: Collapse to icon, expand on focus

notes: Form-associated — participates in native <form> submission.
Ref exposes focus() and clear() methods.

components.title: File Upload
components.description: File selection with drag-and-drop zone, preview thumbnails, and validation.

props.name: maxSize
props.description: Max file size in bytes (0 = unlimited)

components.title: Tabs
components.description: Organize content into switchable panels. Supports underline, enclosed, and pill styles.
notes: Panel content is an HTML string, not JSX.

components.title: Accordion
components.description: Expandable content sections. Supports single or multiple expanded panels.

props.name: mode
props.description: How many panels can be open

notes: Panel content is an HTML string, not JSX.
Chevron rotation is handled by WASM — direction updates on interaction.

components.title: Breadcrumb
components.description: Hierarchical navigation trail showing the current page location.

components.title: Menu
components.description: Action list triggered by a button. Supports icons, shortcuts, separators, and danger items.

props.name: entries
props.description: { type, id, label, icon?, shortcut?, danger?, disabled? }

components.title: Pagination
components.description: Navigate between pages of content.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the nav element

props.name: showInfo
props.description: Show "Page X of Y" info text

notes: Page navigation is consumer-controlled. Listen to onChange, then update currentPage with the new page value from the event detail.

components.title: Table
components.description: Structured data display with sorting, selection, pagination, row hover, and striping.

props.name: columns
props.description: { id, header, sortable?, width?, align?, pinned? }

props.name: sorts
props.description: Current sort state — [{ column_id, direction }]. Consumer-controlled.

props.name: emptyState
props.description: Content shown when rows is empty

props.name: loading
props.description: Number of skeleton rows to show while loading

props.name: error
props.description: Error message to display instead of rows

notes: Sorting is consumer-controlled. The Table fires onSort but does NOT re-order rows internally. You must listen to onSort, update your rows, and pass the current sort via the sorts prop.
Selection is also consumer-controlled. Listen to onSelect and pass selected row IDs back via the selected prop.
Pagination is consumer-controlled. Listen to onPage and update the pagination prop with the new current_page.
Cells accept either an array of TableCell objects (positional, matching column order) or a Record keyed by column id.

components.title: Listbox
components.description: Scrollable selectable option list with keyboard navigation.

components.title: Stepper
components.description: Multi-step progress indicator with status per step. Supports horizontal and vertical layout.

components.title: Profile Menu
components.description: Avatar-triggered dropdown menu for user account actions.

props.name: entries
props.description: { type, id, label, icon?, shortcut?, danger?, disabled? }

notes: Ref exposes open() and close() methods.

components.title: Alert
components.description: Contextual feedback messages for user actions. Supports icons, dismiss, and action buttons.

components.title: Toast
components.description: Temporary notification messages that auto-dismiss.

components.title: Progress
components.description: Visual indicator of completion percentage.
notes: value is always 0-100 (percentage). No max prop.
Use ::part(fill) to style the progress bar.

components.title: Spinner
components.description: Loading indicator for async operations.

components.title: Skeleton
components.description: Placeholder shapes while content loads. Supports text, circle, and rectangular variants.

components.title: Thinking
components.description: Animated thinking/loading indicator with shimmer effect. Designed for AI chat interfaces.

components.title: Dialog
components.description: Modal overlay for focused interactions. Traps focus, closes on Escape or backdrop click.

components.title: Drawer
components.description: Slide-in panel from screen edge. Useful for navigation, settings, or detail views.

props.name: side
props.description: Edge the drawer slides in from

events.name: onClose
events.description: Closed via backdrop, Escape, or close button

notes: Supports both declarative (open prop) and imperative (ref.open() / ref.close()) control.
Closes automatically on backdrop click, Escape key, or close button. Listen to onClose to sync your state.
Animations respect prefers-reduced-motion. Enter: 400ms slide, Exit: 250ms slide.

components.title: Popover
components.description: Floating content anchored to a trigger element.

components.title: Tooltip
components.description: Contextual information on hover or focus. Appears above the trigger element.
notes: children and trigger are interchangeable — if both provided, trigger wins.
Accessible name comes from content prop.

components.title: Backdrop
components.description: Overlay layer for modals and drawers with tint, blur, and click-to-close support.
notes: Used internally by Dialog and Drawer. Can be used standalone for custom overlays.

components.title: Message Bubble
components.description: Chat message container with sender alignment, sender metadata, and timestamp.
notes: Use Message Bubble for simple sender-aligned chat messages.
Start with the /docs/messages guide if you need to understand how it relates to Message Group and Message Part.

components.title: Message Group
components.description: Grouped sequence of message parts — text, code blocks, tool calls, and thinking indicators.
notes: Use Message Group when one assistant turn contains multiple typed parts such as text, code, tool activity, and thinking.
For grouped assistant turns, prefer the declarative MessageGroupPart[] parts array.
MessageGroupPart currently uses snake_case keys like tool_name and thinking_label.
Treat grouped parts as the settled record of an assistant turn rather than a scratch surface for every token.
Start with the /docs/messages guide if you need the conceptual model for the message stack.

components.title: Message Part
components.description: Standalone typed message segment with markdown, code blocks, tool activity, thinking states, errors, and optional streaming.

props.name: content
props.description: Plain text, code, or error content

props.name: toolName
props.description: Tool or function name for tool parts

props.name: toolArguments
props.description: Serialized tool input for tool-call parts

props.name: completed
props.description: Mark a thinking state as completed

props.name: language
props.description: Code language hint for code-block parts

props.name: filename
props.description: Filename shown in the code block header

props.name: connection
props.description: Connected-card position when composing parts manually

props.name: onStreamEnd
props.description: Called after the final sanitizing render pass completes

notes: Message Part can now be used directly for text, code blocks, tool calls, tool results, thinking, and errors.
Standalone Message Part uses camelCase props like toolName, toolStatus, and thinkingLabel.
Start with the /docs/messages guide before using Message Part directly in a larger conversation layout.
Supports streaming markdown rendering via ref methods (startStream, appendTokens, endStream).
Use it as the in-flight surface for streaming or for a single standalone segment, not as a replacement for an entire grouped assistant turn.
Use Message Group when one assistant turn contains several connected parts; use Message Part when one segment should stand alone.

components.title: Activity Group
components.description: Grouped activity feed with expandable entries and status indicators.
notes: Use Activity Group for operational or tool-driven updates around a conversation, not as a replacement for Message Bubble.
Start with the /docs/messages guide if you are designing an assistant or agent interface.
In the current React wrapper, status is still passed explicitly even though the broader message timeline model can derive it upstream.

components.title: Code Block
components.description: Display syntax-highlighted code with optional filename chrome, line numbers, traffic lights, and inline copy.

props.name: filename
props.description: Optional filename shown in the title bar

props.name: trafficLights
props.description: Show macOS-style traffic lights in the title bar

notes: Use copyButton to enable built-in copy feedback with an inline checkmark.
Use trafficLights for terminal-style framing in docs and chat surfaces.
Minimal variant is useful for inline docs sections where full chrome would be too heavy.
